description:
You are an edge-computing engineer deploying updated code to a fleet of IoT devices.  
    In this sandbox one device is represented by the local directory  
    /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/  

    A freshly-built release is waiting in  
    /home/user/edge_source/  

    Your goal is to **synchronise** the two trees with a *single* terminal command that copies every new
    or modified file from the source to the destination **and** removes anything in the destination that
    no longer exists in the source.

    After the operation the following conditions must all be true:

    1. File /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/version.txt must contain exactly  
       1.1.0⏎   (a newline after the number).
    2. File /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/config.yaml must exist and contain the line  
       setting: prod
    3. File /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/old.tmp must no longer be present.
    4. Create the directory /home/user/deployment_logs/ (if it does not yet exist) and inside it
       create the log file /home/user/deployment_logs/sync_2024.log with **exactly** the
       three lines shown below, each terminated by a single UNIX newline (⏎):

       SYNC COMPLETE⏎  
       SOURCE: /home/user/edge_source/⏎  
       DESTINATION: /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/⏎  

    Notes & constraints:

    • Use only one shell command to perform the actual file synchronisation operation  
      (pre-creating the log directory and writing the log file may require separate commands).  
    • Do not modify the paths, capitalisation, or text shown above; the automated
      grader checks them byte-for-byte.  
    • You do *not* have SSH access; treat the “remote” device as the local directory given.  
    • Do not run commands with sudo. Everything must be achievable as the normal user.  
    • When you are finished, the grader will verify the four numbered conditions exactly.  
      Any deviation (extra spaces, missing newline, wrong file contents, etc.) will be
      considered a failure.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:

    Source tree (/home/user/edge_source/)
    ├── app.py               - contents: print("Edge app v1.1.0")\n
    ├── version.txt          - contents: 1.1.0\n
    └── config.yaml          - contents: setting: prod\n

    Destination tree (/home/user/iot_device/edge_app/)
    ├── app.py               - contents: print("Edge app v1.0.0")\n
    ├── version.txt          - contents: 1.0.0\n
    └── old.tmp              - contents: legacy\n

    No directory /home/user/deployment_logs/ exists yet.

    Expected final state after successful completion:

    /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/
    ├── app.py               - contents: print("Edge app v1.1.0")\n               (updated)
    ├── version.txt          - contents: 1.1.0\n                                  (updated)
    ├── config.yaml          - contents: setting: prod\n                           (new file)
    (file old.tmp no longer present)                                              (deleted)

    /home/user/deployment_logs/sync_2024.log
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │SYNC COMPLETE\n                                         │
    │SOURCE: /home/user/edge_source/\n                      │
    │DESTINATION: /home/user/iot_device/edge_app/\n         │
    └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    The grader will:
      • Confirm the three exact lines (with trailing newlines) in the log file.
      • Confirm the contents and presence/absence of the files listed above.
name: task_000000_9d99c932
